dashboard.label: Search Scope
dashboard.description: Choose the default base search used by the Suricata dashboards.

search.query: | inputlookup suricata_settings.csv | stats latest(eval(if(setting="search_scope", value, null()))) as search_scope latest(eval(if(setting="time_choice", value, null()))) as time_choice | eval search_scope=coalesce(search_scope, "index=suricata") | eval time_choice=coalesce(time_choice, "24h") | eval earliest_tok=case(time_choice=="7d", "-7d@d", time_choice=="30d", "-30d@d", time_choice=="all", "0", true(), "-24h@h") | eval latest_tok="now"

html.p: The dashboards in this app now accept a Search Scope value. The default is index=suricata, but you can also use values like index=pfsense, source=eve.json or index=suricata source="eve.json".
Use Save search scope and time range to make these values the defaults when dashboards are opened again. Other dashboards can still be changed temporarily for one investigation.

search.query: | makeresults count=2 | where "$save_scope$"="1" | streamstats count as row | eval setting=if(row==1, "search_scope", "time_choice") | eval value=if(row==1, $search_scope|s$, $time_choice|s$) | table setting value | outputlookup suricata_settings.csv | stats count as saved_settings | eval status="Saved default" | eval saved_search_scope=$search_scope|s$ | eval saved_time_range=$time_choice|s$ | table status saved_search_scope saved_time_range saved_settings
